chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الشُّهُورِ الَّتِي تُسْتَحَبُّ فِيهَا الْعُمْرَةُ وَ مَنْ أَحْرَمَ فِي شَهْرٍ وَ أَحَلَّ فِي آخَرَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 208 – The months in which the Umra is recommended, and the one who wears Ihraam in one month and frees in another

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/236)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/236)
text: كَانَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا أَرَادَ الْعُمْرَةَ انْتَظَرَ إِلَى صَبِيحَةِ ثَلَاثٍ وَ عِشْرِينَ مِنْ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ ثُمَّ يَخْرُجُ مُهِلًّا فِي ذَلِكَ الْيَوْمِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Hammad Bin Usman who said,
‘It was so that when Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> performed the Umra until the morning of the twenty-third from a Month of Ramazaan, then he<sup>asws</sup> went out commencing during that day’.
en.sarwar: 4. Al-FIusayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-FIassan ibn Ali from Flammad ibn ‘Uthaman who has said the following: “When abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, wanted to perform al-‘Umrah he (the Imam) would wait until the morning of the twenty-third of the month of Ramadan, then saying Talbiyah leave on that day.” (Fatwa best explains Hadith 1-4 of this Chapter)
